items.description: Large ornate initial capital at the head of the text with elaborate internal pen-flourishes, loops, and a central circular motif.
items.scholarly_note: Standard Gothic bookhand decorative initial by the main scribe marking the start of a major textual division (Frá dauða Sinfjötla / Helgakviða Hundingsbana II section).

items.description: Marginal annotation 'p.9' written level with an upper line of text.
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval scholarly reference mark, likely stanza, page or line 9 from a printed edition or collation of the Poetic Edda.

items.description: Marginal annotation 'a.9' written beside the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Later reference mark; 'a' probably denotes a section or witness group with line/stanza 9 in an editorial numbering system.

items.description: Marginal annotation '.j.9' (with preceding dot) beside the text.
items.scholarly_note: Editorial line or stanza reference; 'j' may be a sigla for a manuscript witness, variant, or stanza identifier.

items.description: Marginal annotation '4.j' beside a lower section of text.
items.scholarly_note: Reference mark in the same annotating hand, possibly stanza 4 of section j or equivalent editorial system.

items.description: Marginal annotation 'j.4' (appears twice at different heights in lower half).
items.scholarly_note: Additional line/stanza reference marks belonging to the same later annotation system.

items.description: Numeral '43.' (or '43,') at top right corner outside the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Older pagination or foliation mark added before modern foliation; common in the manuscript's post-medieval history.

items.description: Dark crossed strokes or 'X'-like mark near the top of the opening line, intruding into or above the first line of text.
items.scholarly_note: Possible cancellation, deletion, or reader's nota mark; could also be an accidental pen stroke or early annotation.

items.description: Multiple letters and words within the main text show overwriting, denser ink, or altered letter forms indicating in-place fixes.
items.scholarly_note: Scribal self-corrections by the main hand during copying; typical of active emendation rather than later intervention.

items.description: Pronounced brown staining, darkening, and ragged parchment loss at the lower right corner with irregular torn edge.
items.scholarly_note: Cumulative physical damage from handling, age, and possible moisture; affects only the extreme lower outer corner.

scratches: Faint linear abrasions and surface wear visible on the vellum, especially lower half and near the damaged corner (hair-side preparation marks or use wear).
Minor surface disruptions and creases that do not penetrate the ink layer in several places within and beside the text block.
damage_notes: Lower right corner of the leaf is torn with significant parchment loss and irregular ragged edge.
General age-related staining and discoloration throughout, heaviest in lower and outer areas; consistent with iron-gall ink and handling.
Some show-through and faint offset/ghost text from the verso visible in thinner areas of the vellum.
Edge wear, cockling, and minor abrasions typical of a well-used 13th-century Icelandic manuscript leaf.
summary: Page 52 of Codex Regius (GKS 2365 4to) contains text of Frá dauða Sinfjötla and the opening of Helgakviða Hundingsbana II in the main Icelandic Gothic book hand on well-prepared calf vellum. The page is free of faces, animals, whimsical doodles, or pen trials. Visible non-text features comprise one large decorative flourished initial at the head of the text, a series of later right-margin editorial reference marks (p.9, a.9, .j.9, 4.j, j.4), an older '43.' pagination numeral, a dark crossed mark near the top of the opening line, scattered scribal corrections within the text block, and a prominent capital 'K' beginning the final lines. Physical condition is generally good apart from characteristic lower-right corner loss, staining, and age-related surface wear with minor offset from the verso.
